q: An object that is accelerating must have a ___ force acting on it.
The force applied to keep an object moving in a circle is known as the ___ force.
In the absence of centripetal force, the object would move in a ___ line.
The magnitude of the centripetal force is $F_c = m a_c = ___$.
Substituting $v = r \omega$, the centripetal force is $F_c = ___$.
The ___ in the string provides the required centripetal force for a ball to keep moving in circular path.
The ___ attractive force between the nucleus and the electron acts as the required centripetal force.
On a flat road, the centripetal force is supplied by ___ between the tires and the road.
Banking of roads is raising the ___ edge of the curved road higher than the inner edge.
The normal force $F_N$ exerted by a banked road is ___ to the road.
The ___ component of normal force is the required centripetal force.
Since there is no vertical motion, the net force on car in ___ direction is zero.
The banking angle of a road $\theta$ is chosen so that $\tan \theta = ___$.
The upward thrust (lifting force) acts ___ to an aeroplane body.
The horizontal component of the ___ force on the aeroplane provides the centripetal force.
Centripetal force is the force acting ___ the centre of the circular path.
Centrifugal force appears to act in a direction pointing ___ from the centre.
The outward force a person feels in a moving car turning a curve is the centrifugal force caused by the effect of ___.
Centrifugal force is an ___ or fictitious force which is equal and opposite to the centripetal force.
A ___ is a piece of equipment that puts an object in rotation around a fixed axis.
Low density substances ___ to the top in a laboratory centrifuge.
The effect where denser particles settle to the bottom is known as ___ principle.
Centrifugal pump is a ___ machine which converts mechanical energy into hydraulic energy.
The rotating ___ moves water or other fluids by using centrifugal force.
In a washing machine, excess water is removed with the help of ___ force.
The centrifugal governor is a device used to measure and ___ the speed of a machine.
Centrifugal force results from the effect of ___.
A satellite orbiting a planet is supported by the ___ force.
Removing excess water by ___ drying clothes is with the help of centrifugal force.
The centrifugal governor balls revolve around the ___ at a certain speed.
